aiashley — global digest Generated 07:59 UTC

Sunday, July 12, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

Escalating tensions in the Persian Gulf dominated headlines as the US launched strikes on Iran after Tehran attacked a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz and declared the waterway closed [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. Iran subsequently struck targets in the UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain, with the IRGC warning of a 'crushing response' to further US aggression [Al Jazeera]. The crisis comes days after President Trump suggested interim negotiations were 'over.' In Washington, the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham at 71 sent shockwaves through the political establishment [France 24, BBC, RNZ]. Graham, who had just returned from Kyiv meeting President Zelensky, was a leading voice on foreign policy and chairman of the Senate Budget Committee. In Asia, Typhoon Bavi made landfall in eastern China after forcing nearly two million evacuations across Zhejiang province [BBC, France 24]. Southern Europe continued to battle extreme heat, with Spain reporting at least 12 deaths in one of its deadliest wildfires near Almería, while France's Eiffel Tower and Louvre closed early amid a severe heatwave [BBC Europe, RFI]. At the World Cup, Argentina defeated Switzerland 3-1 in extra time to advance to the semifinals [France 24].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

US Launches Strikes on Iran After Ship Attack; Tehran Closes Strait of Hormuz

  • Iran's IRGC attacked a Cyprus-flagged container ship (MV GFS Galaxy) in the Strait of Hormuz, setting it ablaze and forcing crew evacuation; one Indian national remains missing
  • US Central Command responded with strikes on 140 Iranian military targets including missile sites, drone facilities, and communication networks
  • Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and launched retaliatory strikes against US bases and Gulf Arab states including UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain
  • The escalation follows Trump's suggestion that interim Iran deal negotiations were 'over' and marks the third round of US-Iran strikes this week
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasized closure of strait and scale of US response France 24Provided live blog coverage with emphasis on regional spillover to Oman Globe and MailContextualized within broader energy crisis and failed negotiations Financial TimesHeadline focused on strait closure Al JazeeraHighlighted IRGC's vow of 'crushing response' and framed US actions as aggression
02 Geopolitics

US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71 After Sudden Illness

  • Republican Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina died Saturday evening from a 'brief and sudden illness' according to his office
  • Graham, 71, had just returned from Kyiv where he met President Zelensky on Friday to work on Russia sanctions legislation
  • He served as chairman of the Senate Budget Committee and was one of Washington's most influential foreign policy voices
  • Graham was a strong supporter of Israel and advocate for US support to Ukraine; Israeli President Herzog called him 'a beacon of moral clarity'
Coverage
France 24Emphasized his role as Trump ally and Israel supporter BBC WorldNoted his recent Ukraine trip and evolution from Trump critic to supporter RNZFocused on his defense policy positions Al JazeeraHeadline emphasized his role as 'one of Israel's strongest US Senate allies' Financial Times
03 Climate

Typhoon Bavi Makes Landfall in Eastern China, Nearly 2 Million Evacuated

  • Typhoon Bavi made landfall in Zhejiang province Saturday evening with winds of 144 km/h before weakening to a severe tropical storm
  • Nearly 1.7 million people were evacuated in Zhejiang with thousands more in neighboring provinces
  • The storm previously killed at least 17 people in the Philippines and left tens of thousands without power in Taiwan and Japan
  • China had already experienced deadly storms in the past week leaving 39 dead and causing reservoir failures
Coverage
France 24Emphasized scale of evacuations and prior week's flooding deaths BBC WorldNoted storm's size comparable to width of France; detailed regional impact
04 Climate

Spain Battles One of Its Deadliest Wildfires as Heat Grips Southern Europe

  • At least 12 people killed in wildfire near Bedar in Almería region, including four Britons; 23 people still missing
  • Fire has burned 6,600 hectares; cause attributed to a fallen power line though electricity companies deny responsibility
  • France experiencing its third heatwave since May, with 24 departments under maximum red alert and temperatures reaching 40°C
  • Eiffel Tower, Louvre, and Musée d'Orsay closing early; several towns cancelling Bastille Day fireworks
Coverage
BBC EuropeFocused on British casualties and search operations RFI EnglishEmphasized impact on Paris tourism and national holiday events
05 Policy

Trump Administration Subpoenas New York Times Reporters Over Air Force One Report

  • Department of Justice subpoenaed multiple NYT journalists after they reported on security concerns with the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One
  • Federal agents delivered subpoenas to reporters' homes; journalists ordered to testify before federal grand jury in Manhattan
  • Subpoenas issued after FBI Director Kash Patel and DOJ officials met at White House on Friday
  • Trump used older Air Force One model to leave NATO summit in Turkey, citing threats from Iran
Coverage
Globe and MailDescribed it as 'dramatic escalation' of Trump's campaign against media; emphasized erosion of press freedom
Also Today
  • Argentina defeats Switzerland 3-1 in extra time to reach World Cup semifinals, setting up clash with England France 24
  • England beats Norway 2-1 in extra time on Bellingham brace to advance to World Cup semifinals RFI English
  • At least two killed in shooting at Toronto salsa festival; police say gunfire exchanged between two individuals BBC World
  • Polish PM Tusk announces national memorial to victims of WWII 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists,' urging Ukraine to embrace truth for EU membership BBC Europe
  • Germany agrees deal to buy US Tomahawk missiles as stopgap while Europe develops own defense systems RFI English
  • Police arrest 28-year-old suspect in killing of UK politician Le Monde
  • Man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane window mid-flight after it dislodged; passenger rescued by wife BBC Europe
  • Linda Noskova defeats Karolina Muchova in dramatic all-Czech Wimbledon final after recovering from five missed championship points BBC Europe
  • Russia strikes kill eight across Ukraine including five in Sumy; Zelensky calls for faster weapons deliveries Globe and Mail
  • Nigeria says army killed over 300 bandits in two-day operation in Zamfara state The Guardian
